search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG61 IBS bloating bowel habit abdominal discomfort symptom profile
site:dha.gov.ae abdominal pain adults bloating red flags telehealth
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diet, medicine, laboratory test, imaging, endoscopy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Bloating has gastrointestinal, gynaecological, urinary, dietary and systemic causes.
No autonomous investigation or management is mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with chronic bloating in an IBS-symptom context; children, acute distension, pregnancy, obstruction and systemic illness require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, imaging-result, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: UAE DHA evidence applies where cited; international gastroenterology and hepatology evidence requires UAE laboratory, imaging, referral, alcohol-service, prescribing, and local pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult IBS symptom-profile section supports duration, bloating, pain or discomfort, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation, stool frequency or form, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus and impact without diagnosing IBS.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician assessment and examination for red-flag context without populating a negative or asserting disease.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports clinician-entered follow-up and safety-net documentation when arranged without setting an interval or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute versus chronic course, distension, diet, pregnancy, gynaecological and urinary context, cause, diagnosis, investigation, and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
